title: Ambiguous type
explanation: This error occurs when multiple trait implementations could apply, and the compiler cannot determine which one to use. This typically happens with methods that are defined on multiple traits that a type implements.
error_message: error[E0283]: type annotations needed — cannot resolve ambiguity

fixes.description: Use fully qualified syntax to disambiguate
fixes.code:
Greet::hello(&p);  // Calls Greet's hello
Meet::hello(&p);   // Calls Meet's hello

fixes.description: Use angle bracket syntax for full disambiguation

tip: Fully qualified syntax <Type as Trait>::method() is the universal way to resolve any method ambiguity in Rust.
